event: heart rate was 122 beats per min
lying in bed because of chills and nausea
chest compression and tracheal intubation were immediately initiated
ECG showed a “coved-type” ST elevation in V1 and V2
this was improved when the patient's fever subsided with acetaminophen
suspected septic shock from ulcerative colitis exacerbation
a contrast-enhanced CT scan revealed a round low-density area of his liver
determined to be a liver abscess
changed the antibiotics to meropenem and vancomycin
the cardiologist confirmed a positive pilsicainide test result
implantation of an implantable cardioverter defibrillator (ICD) was performed
one of his maternal uncles had experienced sudden death
an endocrinologist examined the causes of HC
was associated with high parathyroid hormone levels
on investigation with Tc scintigraphy, abnormal uptake in the anterior mediastinum was observed
ECG and serum calcium level were followed up and treated
Pathological diagnosis was determined for an ectopic parathyroid adenoma
CT and magnetic resonance imaging revealed tumors in the pituitary and adrenal glands
was finally presumed to have multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1)
